2605.30187.title: Modularizing Educational LLM-Agency for Fostering Responsible Learning Assistance
2605.30187.paper_summary: The widespread adoption of AI chatbots in education will drastically change learning, making responsible deployment a critical concern. While large language models (LLMs) might have access to sources discussing insights from educational sciences, they are not particularly inclined to adhere to pedagogical concepts, risking negative effects on the learning process, such as a loss of transfer capabilities, critical thinking, or creativity. In this paper, we introduce an agentic AI chatbot architecture assisting students with exercise solving, specifically designed to contribute to more responsible AI use in education. We base our conceptual development on the identification of several desiderata for responsible LLM-based educational systems, argue for the structural shortcomings inherent in monolithic, out-of-the-box solutions, and instead suggest modularizing the agentic architecture. We propose specific modules for different stages of exercise solving, enabling incorporation of targeted pedagogical advice, guiding students through the learning process in a more controllable, transparent, and overseeable manner.
2605.30187.paper_summary_zh: 教育中 AI 聊天機器人的廣泛採用將徹底改變學習，使得負責任的部署成為一個關鍵問題。雖然大型語言模型 (LLMs) 可能能夠訪問討論教育科學見解的來源，但它們並不特別傾向於遵循教學概念，這可能會對學習過程產生負面影響，例如轉移能力、批判性思維或創造力的喪失。在本文中，我們介紹了一種代理 AI 聊天機器人架構，幫助學生解決練習問題，特別設計用於促進教育中更負責任的 AI 使用。我們的概念發展基於對負責任的 LLM 基礎教育系統的幾個期望的識別，主張單一的、即用型解決方案固有的結構缺陷，並建議對代理架構進行模組化。我們提出針對不同練習解決階段的具體模組，使得能夠納入針對性的教學建議，以更可控、透明和可監督的方式引導學生通過學習過程。
2605.30187.author: Julius Gabelmann et.al.
2605.30187.authors: Julius Gabelmann, Felix Jahn, Kevin Baum, Sophie van Rossum, Emely Wuenscher, Timo P. Gros, Verena Wolf
